hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model jointly aligning SMILES strings with 3D electron-density grids is trained using symmetric InfoNCE and SigLIP losses, then the resulting molecular embeddings will be invariant to arbitrary rigid-body rotations of the molecule, enabling rotation-agnostic retrieval and stable downstream property predictions across rotated poses, because cross-modal contrastive objectives enforce pose-invariant alignment between SMILES and 3D density representations.
